content:
This is a marketplace app for renting heavy equipment between individuals and companies in Saudi Arabia.

Project name:
Heavyar

Important:
Use the attached Heavyar logo / app icon as the ONLY branding source.
Do NOT generate any logos, icons, mascots, or replacement branding.
Do NOT invent a different visual identity.
Use this official logo for:
- app icon
- splash screen
- auth header / branding
- key brand moments in the UI

Extract the color system from the logo and apply it consistently:
- deep navy blue
- gold / yellow accents

Build the app in Expo / React Native for BOTH Android and iOS.

Core app concept:
Users can:
- browse heavy equipment
- search and filter listings
- open equipment details
- send rental requests
- receive or manage requests
- chat inside each request
- pay inside the app
- rate providers after completion

Main business flow:
1. User signs up / logs in
2. User browses equipment
3. User opens equipment detail
4. User sends rental request
5. Provider accepts or rejects
6. Customer opens request detail and can chat with provider inside that request
7. Customer pays inside the app
8. Request moves forward
9. Provider completes request
10. Customer rates provider

Critical product requirements:
- Arabic is the DEFAULT language
- English is the secondary language
- The app must launch in Arabic by default
- Arabic mode must use full RTL layout correctly across the entire app
- When switching to English, the app must switch to full LTR layout correctly across the entire app
- This must behave like modern global apps: layout direction, text alignment, icons, paddings, navigation flow, and UI mirroring must adapt correctly to the selected language
- Do not make Arabic just translated text on an LTR layout
- Do not make English just translated text on an RTL layout
- The language switch must feel fully native and professional in both directions
- Single flexible user account
- Same user can browse, request, and list equipment
- Clean modern Saudi-friendly UI
- Dark modern theme using navy + gold from the official logo
- Bottom tab navigation

Do NOT use Supabase.
Keep the structure ready for Firebase integration later.

Do NOT build the real backend yet.
For now, use mock/local data only, but structure the app to be Firebase-ready.

Also prepare the architecture for:
- Firebase Auth
- Firestore
- FCM notifications
- Cloudinary uploads
- Tap payment integration

Do NOT hardcode any real credentials.

Required mobile screens in this first phase:
- Splash
- Onboarding
- Login
- Register
- Home / Browse
- Search / Filters
- Equipment Detail
- Create Equipment Listing
- My Equipment
- Requests
- Request Detail
- Chat
- Payment
- Rating
- Profile
- Settings

UI and flow requirements:
- Bottom tab bar navigation
- Home should include categories, featured items, and recent listings
- Equipment detail should include pricing, location, owner info, and request button
- Requests should support statuses:
  pending, accepted, in_progress, completed, rejected, cancelled
- Chat must be tied to request ID
- Chat should be open only while request is active
- Chat must show closed state when request is completed / rejected / cancelled
- Payment UI must be Tap-ready only, not real integration yet
- Rating should be available only after completed requests

Data model structure should stay ready for Firebase:
- users
- equipment
- equipmentRequests
- equipmentRequests/{requestId}/messages
- ratings
- appSettings
- adminUsers

Equipment fields should be designed for:
- ownerUid
- titleAr / titleEn
- descriptionAr / descriptionEn
- category
- city
- district
- location
- pricePerDay
- images
- availability
- isActive

Request fields should be designed for:
- equipmentId
- customerUid
- providerUid
- status
- startDate
- endDate
- notes
- amount
- platformFee
- providerAmount
- paymentStatus
- paymentId
- paidAt
- currency
- allowChat

Payment status values:
- unpaid
- pending_payment
- paid
- failed
- refunded

Structure requirements:
- clean reusable components
- no messy architecture
- no fake placeholder branding
- no restart after approval
- every approval should be treated as a locked checkpoint
- continue incrementally from the latest approved state
- do not regenerate completed parts unless necessary

For this first phase:
Build the app foundation, navigation, theme, localization, reusable UI components, and full screen flow using mock data.

At the end, provide:
1. screen inventory
2. navigation structure
3. reusable components created
4. data model summary
5. what is fully built now
6. what is prepared for future Firebase / Tap / Cloudinary integration
